skill.name: twitter-automation
skill.description: Automate Twitter/X with posting, engagement, and user management via inference.sh CLI. Apps: x/post-tweet, x/post-create (with media), x/post-like, x/post-retweet, x/dm-send, x/user-follow. Capabilities: post tweets, schedule content, like posts, retweet, send DMs, follow users, get profiles. Use for: social media automation, content scheduling, engagement bots, audience growth, X API. Triggers: twitter api, x api, tweet automation, post to twitter, twitter bot, social media automation, x automation, tweet scheduler, twitter integration, post tweet, twitter post, x post, send tweet
skill.summary: Post tweets, engage with content, and manage followers via the belt CLI and inference.sh platform.
skill.author: inference-sh-skills

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 37 external_command patterns (backtick usage), 7 hardcoded URLs, and 2 weak cryptographic algorithm warnings across 1 file (SKILL.md, 158 lines). All findings are FALSE POSITIVES: backtick usage is markdown code fence syntax in documentation code blocks, URLs reference the legitimate inference.sh platform, and the crypto warnings stem from YAML front matter and a table header. The skill is documentation-only showing CLI usage of the belt tool. No executable code, no prompt injection, and no malicious intent detected.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detection
high_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged YAML front matter description (line 3) and a markdown table header (line 29) as cryptographic algorithm usage. SKILL.md contains no cryptographic operations. These are false positives from pattern matching on markdown syntax.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: External Command Execution via Backticks
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 37 backtick usages as Ruby/shell execution. All instances are markdown formatting: fenced code blocks (```bash) documenting belt CLI commands, and inline backticks for app IDs like x/post-tweet. No actual shell execution exists in this documentation-only skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hardcoded Network URLs
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 7 URLs as potential network calls. All URLs reference the legitimate inference.sh platform documentation, a banner image hosted on inference.sh, a GitHub raw content URL for install instructions, and an example URL in a code comment. No actual network requests originate from the skill itself.

content.user_title: Automate Twitter/X posts and engagement from your terminal
content.value_statement: Managing Twitter/X manually wastes time and slows down your content strategy. This skill lets you post tweets, like content, send DMs, and manage followers directly from your AI assistant using the belt CLI.
actual_capabilities: Post text tweets and media-rich content to Twitter/X using the belt CLI
Like, retweet, and delete tweets by ID with a single command
Send direct messages to individual users through the x/dm-send app
Follow users and retrieve user profile information and tweet details
Combine with AI image and video generation for end-to-end content creation workflows
limitations: Requires belt CLI to be installed and authenticated with inference.sh
Requires a Twitter/X API integration configured in the inference.sh dashboard
Cannot read timelines, search tweets, or access direct message history
Commands execute immediately with no built-in scheduling or queuing

use_cases.title: Schedule and automate Twitter/X posting
use_cases.description: Post tweets, share media, and engage with content at scale through automated CLI commands.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers handling multiple brand accounts

use_cases.title: Integrate Twitter into development workflows
use_cases.description: Post deployment updates, bug fix announcements, and release notes directly from CI/CD pipelines.

use_cases.title: Cross-post AI-generated content
use_cases.description: Generate images and videos with AI models and immediately share them on Twitter/X.

prompt_templates.title: Post a simple tweet
prompt_templates.prompt: Post a new tweet that says 'Hello from my AI assistant!'
prompt_templates.scenario: Posting your first tweet through the belt CLI

prompt_templates.title: Post a tweet with media attachment
prompt_templates.prompt: Post a tweet with the text 'Check out this photo' and attach the image at https://example.com/photo.jpg
prompt_templates.scenario: Sharing visual content with a tweet

prompt_templates.title: Like and retweet a post
prompt_templates.prompt: Like tweet 1234567890 and then retweet it to your followers.
prompt_templates.scenario: Engaging with content from other users

prompt_templates.title: Generate AI art and share on Twitter
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an AI image of a sunset over the ocean, get the result URL, and post it to Twitter with the caption 'AI-generated art of a sunset.'

output_examples.input: Post a tweet saying 'Just launched my new project!'
output_examples.output: Tweet posted successfully. View it at: https://x.com/username/status/1234567890123456789

output_examples.input: Show me the profile of user OpenAI
output_examples.output: User: OpenAI (@OpenAI) - 5.2M followers, following 387. Bio: Research lab. Joined December 2015.

output_examples.input: Send a DM to user_123 saying 'Thanks for the follow!'
output_examples.output: Direct message sent to user_123 successfully.

best_practices: Verify tweet text for spelling, accuracy, and formatting before posting
Store tweet IDs from responses so you can delete or reply to them later
Use descriptive tweet text with relevant hashtags for better visibility
anti_patterns: Sending unsolicited direct messages to multiple users in bulk
Posting duplicate content without checking for existing tweets on the same topic
Hardcoding sensitive credentials like API keys or access tokens in prompts

faq.question: What is the belt CLI?
faq.answer: Belt is a command-line tool from inference.sh that manages and runs AI applications. This skill uses belt to interact with the Twitter/X API.

faq.question: Do I need a Twitter/X API key?
faq.answer: You need an inference.sh account with the Twitter/X integration enabled. The belt CLI handles authentication through your inference.sh session.

faq.question: Can I schedule tweets for future posting?
faq.answer: This skill posts tweets immediately. For scheduled posting, use an external cron job or task scheduler to trigger belt commands at specific times.

faq.question: How do I post images or videos?
faq.answer: Use the x/post-create app with a media_url parameter pointing to your image or video file URL. The media must be accessible via a public URL.

faq.question: Can I read my Twitter timeline?
faq.answer: No, this skill only supports direct actions like posting, liking, and following. It cannot read timelines, search tweets, or fetch your home feed.

faq.question: What are the rate limits?
faq.answer: Rate limits depend on your inference.sh plan and the Twitter/X API tier. Check the inference.sh documentation for current limits.
